description: Create a content slide on AI’s impact on risk teams with a left sidebar narrative and a right-side horizontal bar chart showing expected workforce impacts over the next three years.

header.headline: AI’s impact on risk teams – productivity gains, more specialists and ongoing evaluation
header.sub: Results point to three primary expectations for AI’s impact on risk teams: improved productivity within a stable headcount, increased hiring of specialist expertise and continued evaluation as use cases mature.

sidebar.body: Results point to three primary expectations for AI’s impact on risk teams: improved productivity within a stable headcount, increased hiring of specialist expertise and continued evaluation as use cases mature.

main.title: Figure 17: Expected impact of AI on the size and composition of risk management workforce in next three years

series.label: Stable workforce size with enhanced productivity (e.g., AI boosts efficiency without significantly altering headcount due to redeploying staff to strategic roles)
Shift to hire specialized expertise as greater needs for specialists in areas like cyber, climate, quantum and AI arise (e.g., due to leveraging AI tools to manage more traditional risks)
Shift to smaller, specialized tech-savvy teams (e.g., due to AI enabling more efficient operations)
Shift to diamond-shaped resource structure vs. traditional pyramid-shaped structure (e.g., due to use of AI for basic analytical needs)
Increased outsourcing of risk management activities (e.g., leveraging third-party vendors for specialized or routine tasks, enabled by AI)
Increased offshoring of risk management activities (e.g., leveraging location strategic/GCCs and AI capabilities to reduce operational costs)
Significant headcount reduction (e.g., due to AI automating repetitive tasks such as data collection, reporting)

labels: Stable workforce size with enhanced productivity (e.g., AI boosts efficiency without significantly altering headcount due to redeploying staff to strategic roles)

Significant headcount reduction (e.g., due to AI automating repetitive tasks such as data collection, reporting)

footer.source: 15th annual EY/IIF global bank risk management survey
